subject: Computer Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 6: ALGORITHMS
topics: This topic introduces learners to the fundamental principles of algorithms. Learners will explore how to
design, represent, and refine step-by-step procedures to solve computational and real-world problems.
Emphasis is placed on algorithmic thinking, logic development, and the use of visual tools like flowcharts
activities: Classroom arranged for interactive discussion and pair work
Open space at the front for demonstration (role-play of the nshima-making algorithm)
Projector/board to display examples of algorithms (recipes, routines, GPS routes)
Optional digital devices for viewing additional examples
resources: Computer Science Teaching Module – Form 2 | Ministry of Education, Zambia | 2026
Introduction: This sub-topic introduces algorithms as step-by-step instructions that solve problems.
Teachers should explain the different types of algorithms and discuss their key characteristics, helping
learners understand how algorithms work in computing. Guide learners to design flowcharts and write
pseudocode, showing how algorithms can be applied to solve computational problems. Encourage
assessment: Problem-Solving: Identify errors and propose improvements to algorithms
Guided Inquiry: Teacher facilitates linking examples to key characteristics
Present four instruction sets on the board and ask learners to evaluate each against the four algorithm
characteristics (finiteness, definiteness, input/output, effectiveness). Example 1: a good algorithm
(clear steps with a definite end). Example 2: an infinite loop ("Keep counting up from 1" – violates
